source_compilation: docs/Impetus notes compilation/Impetus Release Notes.pdf (p. 58 SuperSet/Analytics · 1 paragraph; pp. 97-100 Ask Impetus context)
section_label_contiguity: OK · §02 · §03 · the page is intentionally thin per spec (only 1 paragraph in source PDF + cross-cutting role).

findings.issue: Audience pills contain double-encoded HTML entities: 'All Leaders &amp; Business Users', 'Data &amp; analytics teams'. Renders as visible 'Leaders & Business Users' in browser, not 'Leaders & Business Users'. Apex-visible character glitch.
findings.fix: Replace '&amp;' with '&' (single-encoded) so it renders as '&'. In source YAML the entity may be stored '&' and the renderer is double-escaping. e.g. 'All Leaders & Business Users', 'Data & analytics teams'.

findings.section: page title vs hero vs data slug
findings.issue: Three different module names co-exist: <title>Impetus Command Center</title>, hero H1 'Impetus Command Center.', eyebrow 'IMPETUS · ANALYTICS · LIVE', sub_title '(Analytics · Real-Time Dashboards · powers Ask Impetus)', PDF section header 'SuperSet / Analytics'. The /impetus/index.html card is also 'Impetus Command Center'. Per recent change note, this is intentional ('analytics is Impetus Command Center'). But the eyebrow still says 'INSTADESK'-style 'ANALYTICS' caps which is the slug, not the brand. Reader sees three labels.
findings.fix: Pick one. Recommended: keep '<title>Impetus Command Center · Impetus</title>' + H1 'Impetus Command Center.' + eyebrow 'IMPETUS · COMMAND CENTER · LIVE' (drop 'ANALYTICS' from eyebrow because the slug ≠ brand). Update breadcrumb crumb to 'Impetus Command Center'.

findings.issue: Cortex page is also titled 'Command Center / Cortex' per spec §2 row 1. Now the /analytics page is also called 'Impetus Command Center'. Two different platforms branded as 'Command Center' creates Apex confusion.
findings.fix: Either (a) rename this page H1 back to a distinct brand — 'SuperSet.' (the actual PDF brand) with sub_title '(Impetus Command Center · Analytics layer)', or (b) explicitly disambiguate in copy: 'The Impetus Command Center analytics layer · sits below Cortex'. Recent change note says hero is 'Impetus Command Center' — flagging because the cross-page conflict is real.

findings.issue: Chip 'No standalone update this quarter' is an absence-claim packaged as a stat chip. Reads negative; chips are signal-bearing. The other two chips are scope claims, not stats.
findings.fix: Drop the 'No standalone update' chip. Move that line to §03's caption text. Keep two real chips: 'Powers Ask Impetus across the platform' + 'Substrate for every Cortex / Command Center dashboard'. Caption can carry: 'Data-plane infrastructure — no standalone Q1 release; updates ship under Cortex.'

findings.issue: Caption '₹22.2 Cr/day, ₹1,442 Cr inventory, vendor OTIF 70.6%' borrows Cortex hero numbers without citation source line on this page. Apex reader cannot verify whether the number lives on this page or upstream.
findings.fix: Add an inline anchor: 'The Cortex hero numbers (₹22.2 Cr/day, ₹1,442 Cr inventory, vendor OTIF 70.6% · see Cortex →) all originate here.' Also acceptable: drop the parenthesised numbers and just say 'Every dashboard surfaced in Cortex traces to here.'

findings.issue: Sub-line '(Analytics · Real-Time Dashboards · powers Ask Impetus)' uses a parenthetical aside that duplicates the eyebrow-slug 'ANALYTICS'. Redundant.
findings.fix: Sub-line: '(Real-time dashboards · powers Ask Impetus)' — drop 'Analytics' since eyebrow already carries it.

findings.issue: <title> is bare 'Impetus Command Center' with no '· Impetus' suffix. Other Impetus subpages use '<Platform> · Impetus' format (e.g., instadesk = 'Support Platform · Impetus', recollect = 'Reconciliation Platform · Impetus').
findings.fix: Change to '<title>Impetus Command Center · Impetus</title>' for consistency.

findings.issue: Per audit framework §2 honesty: page is so thin (only §02 + §03) that an Apex reviewer may read 'Live' as overclaiming for a track that has 'no standalone update this quarter'. The PDF source is 1 paragraph + a forward statement.
findings.fix: Acceptable per recent change note (analytics page is intentionally thin). Suggestion: add an inline sub-pill on hero: 'Live · data-plane substrate' to qualify what 'Live' means here. Avoids the optics of a Live tag with no shipped updates.

strengths: Source-honest framing — page openly says 'No standalone update this quarter' which is rare and credible.
Cross-reference to Cortex is explicit ('substrate for every dashboard surfaced in Cortex / Command Center').
Source claims trace cleanly — PDF p. 58 paragraph reproduced verbatim ('Real-time dashboards covering the full journey... powers the AI assistant's data layer').
Console clean (Tailwind dev warning only); page loads with 0 broken images (no images, by design).

verification_notes.image_loads_200: n/a · 0 images on page (intentional per source PDF lacking screenshots for SuperSet section)
verification_notes.lightbox_works: n/a · no lightbox markup on page (intentional)
verification_notes.console_clean: only standard Tailwind CDN dev warning
verification_notes.section_label_run: §02 · §03 contiguous · §04 What improved + §05 Screenshots intentionally absent (no source content)
verification_notes.page_thin_flag: spec §6.3 explicitly carves out this page as the analytics/SuperSet layer with minimal content; thinness is by design
